tasks.title: Review the codebase (backend/ + frontend/) and write a README.md: what Ledgerdary does, architecture, how to run locally and via Docker/Railway
Added README.md covering architecture, local dev, and Docker/Railway deploy
tasks.note:
✅ What was implemented
Added a root-level README.md documenting: what Ledgerdary does (statement import, document/grocery OCR via Claude, spending/tax/annual dashboards, family expenses), the backend/frontend architecture and single-container deploy model, how to run the backend and frontend locally (env vars, ports, Vite proxy), how to build and run via Docker, and how the Railway deploy is configured.

🔍 What to check in the UI:
- Open README.md at the repo root on GitHub — verify it renders cleanly and the setup steps match your actual .env values.

⚠️ Edge cases / concerns
- While reading App.jsx to write this, found that the app's initial-load useEffect (frontend/src/App.jsx ~line 371-393) calls `store.get(...)` on load, but `store` is never defined/imported anywhere in the file — this throws a ReferenceError on every page load, so `loaded` never becomes true, transactions/documents/grocery-receipts/child-expenses are never fetched from the backend on startup, and budgets/subs/investments/income/childname/accounts never get persisted back to `/api/settings` (their sync effects are gated on `loaded`). Filed as a follow-up task below rather than fixing in this run to keep this run scoped to the README task.

Files changed: README.md

tasks.title: Fix broken data load on startup: App.jsx calls undefined `store.get()` instead of the `api` client, so all persisted data (transactions, documents, grocery receipts, child expenses) fails to load on page refresh and settings never sync back
tasks.note: In frontend/src/App.jsx, the initial useEffect (~line 371) awaits `store.get("ml_tx")`, `store.get("ml_docs")`, etc., but no `store` object exists in the file (it's leftover from an earlier local-storage-based version). This throws a ReferenceError inside an un-caught async IIFE, so `setLoaded(true)` is never reached: `loaded` stays false forever, meaning (1) transactions/documents/grocery-receipts/child-expenses are never fetched from the backend via `api.getTransactions()`/`api.getDocuments()`/`api.getGroceryReceipts()`/`api.getChildExpenses()` on mount, and (2) budgets/subs/investments/income/childName/accounts never get pushed to `/api/settings` since those effects are gated on `loaded`. Net effect: on every fresh page load, the dashboard shows empty/default state even though data exists in Postgres. Fix: replace the `store.get(...)` calls with the corresponding `api.get*()` calls (transactions/documents/grocery receipts/child expenses) and `api.getSetting(key)` calls (budgets/subs/investments/income/childname/accounts), matching the pattern already used elsewhere in the file for writes.

tasks.title: Fixed broken startup data load — App.jsx now uses the real api client instead of an undefined store object
tasks.note:
✅ What was implemented
Replaced every `store.get(...)` call in the mount-time useEffect (frontend/src/App.jsx) with the matching `api.get*()` / `api.getSetting(key)` calls: transactions, documents, grocery receipts, and child expenses now load via their dedicated endpoints, and budgets/subs/investments/income/childname/accounts load via `api.getSetting`. Also fixed a latent bug where `accounts` was written to `/api/settings/accounts` on every change but never read back on load — it's now restored on mount too. Wrapped the whole load in try/catch so a single failed fetch can't block `setLoaded(true)` (which un-gates the settings-sync effects).

🔍 What to check in the UI:
- Import some transactions / add a document, then hard-refresh the page — they should still be there (previously the dashboard would silently reset to empty defaults every time).
- Edit an account balance, refresh — balance should persist (this was silently broken before since the read-back never happened even prior to the store.get bug).
- Open the browser console on load — there should be no ReferenceError.

⚠️ Edge cases / concerns
- None known; verified against a real Postgres-backed instance of the backend with seeded transaction/settings data — the dashboard's transaction count, monthly spend, and settings all reflected the seeded values correctly after a fresh page load, which reproduces the exact failure mode this fixes.

Files changed: frontend/src/App.jsx, .gitignore (added — node_modules/dist/playwright-screenshots were untracked but had no ignore rule)
